reviews.review_description:
I love this game!
I had a lovely stroll through a mysterious and surreal hotel.🏨 
I want to recommend this game as everyone’s first VR experience. 
It’s a smooth and frictionless experience of wonder and surprise. 
My only request for the developers is I wish there was optional content.

items to collect on your hand chain to fill your suitcase, by doing special actions. 
Like making the soup in the kitchen . 

Or hiding a secret path in the garden using the put hands on wall mechanic. 


I would also like to see more sound effects. Like how you can knock on doors.

This game is a great VR experience especially when done in roomscale. It was disorienting in the best way to walk around in such a small space while at the same time traversing a huge hotel. I loved how the vibe turned spooky pretty early on.  Game took maybe 2 hours total. The puzzles are quite easy so don’t play this looking for a puzzle challenge, and the graphics are very simplistic. But I really enjoyed the music and sound design of this game and the story they managed to tell through the environment.

This isn't billed as a horror game but as the game progressed my fascination slowly turned to unease, dread, then horror.  I didn't expect that.  It was an unpleasant, pleasant surprise. I love horror games. 😁

Overall it's a very compelling experience, something only possible in VR, and an untethered headset is the perfect platform for it.  The minimalist art design and excellent music are extremely immersive, when technical issues aren't continually breaking the immersion.

Unfortunately as much as I enjoyed the experience I have to knock off a star for the janky and occasionally very frustrating hand interactions and mostly generic puzzles.

Your in-game hands just never feel natural. They're clumsy, become easily stuck on things, and manipulating controls with them can occasionally be maddeningly difficult. Even just selecting something from the main menu feels off somehow.

Puzzle aficionados will likely be disappointed by how generic and simplistic the puzzles are. For me the puzzles provided a welcome mental break in the latter part of the game, a chance to relax for a minute, (when the controls weren't frustrating me).  I do wish most of them weren't so familiar.  

Despite these issues I feel this game is a must buy for anyone interested in a fascinating and unsettling experience.

This is a great game. Not too hard but comes with new mechanics that are fun ever other level. I was afraid that my playspace would be too small for this as I only have a tiny room but it worked even in roomscale mode. Would be great to implement hand tracking.
Based on the title, I expected a longer game.

Only a handful of games have explored natural movement so far (Unseen Diplomacy, Tea for God, Eye of the Temple),  which is a shame, since it really makes the most of being immersed in VR. This is a very well designed exploration of a similar movement concept, having you explore a very trippy MC Esher-like hotel. 
Couldn't put it down - played through it in one sitting. Highly recommended!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the kind words and the feedback! It really means so much to hear that you appreciate the natural movement. It does make VR way more immersive! Designing this game forced us to rethink how we view and move through space, and we're so excited others get to experience the magic.

reviews.review_title: Great game... but wait for a sale
reviews.review_description:
There are very few games on the quest that actually match your IRL playspace to their in-game space using roomscale. I speak as an author of another such game, though I don't use impossible spaces. The navigation here is the same as in Tea for God and TraVRse, aka impossible spaces. It is very well implemented and only requires a 2m x 2m space. I urge you to play it in roomscale and not stationary to get the full effect. It really isn't anywhere near the same experience in stationary, nothing to do with the game design and everything to do with the impossible spaces concept.

Ok, roomscale nerding out aside, this is a truly clever and fun game with unique puzzles. The challenge is low, but their uniqueness makes up for it. I highly recommend this game.

The only caveat is the price. I rated it 5 stars based on the quality of the game, but if it was up to me I would suggest a price between $10 and $15 due to the short length and no replyability (that I could find). So, wishlist, wait for a sale, then pounce on it! :)

I’ve been looking forward to this game for a long time, as it’s from William Chyr, the creator of the fantastic Manifold Garden. He has a background in physics before moving into art installations (and juggling), so he’s a bit of a polymath and that unique mindset really shows in the games he creates. Manifold Garden took around five years to develop, and you can tell he pours everything into crafting these works of art. Hotel Infinity is no exception.

From the moment you start, you’re completely immersed in its distinctive environment. It constantly plays with your sense of perspective, and the puzzles are cleverly designed, challenging without ever feeling unfair, and incredibly satisfying to solve. Just walking around the hotel and interacting with its objects and shifting spaces is an experience in itself. I’ve taken so many screenshots because some rooms are simply beautiful. It’s a game to be savoured and not rushed.

If you’re interested in environmental design, topology, or what VR can achieve when pushed to its limits by a creative mind, I highly recommend this game. I’m about halfway through and haven’t encountered any major bugs beyond getting my hand briefly stuck behind a curtain. The sound design is excellent as well, and the sense of mystery and slight unease runs through the entire experience.

This kind of roomscale VR game is a niche genre within an already niche medium of VR but more titles really should take advantage of it, the immersion is unmatched and I feel it’s one of the best ways to experience VR. I live in a small two-up two-down house and still managed to set up the space required, though other locomotion options are available if needed. That said, roomscale is absolutely the best way to play.

It’s one of the best VR experiences I’ve had, and more people need to know about it. It was such a strange feeling taking off the headset off after a session and to see the real world looking so normal again, it made me question my own reality! I highly recommend Hotel Infinity. Thank you developers for the wild ride!

Amazing, stunning, magical! This makes me ponder reality... if you like Escher you will love this. Make sure you play it roomscale! Beautiful sound design too. Absolutely psychedelic and unreal in every way.

reviews.review_title: Very trippy and cool game but…
reviews.review_description: Took maybe an hour to beat the entire game, it was a great experience…. But not one worth 20 dollars, game was very well done loved the puzzles and environments I just think realistically for the content you’re offering it’s more of a 5 dollar game, MAYBE 10 and that’s a hard maybe coming from someone who’s been playing vr for 7 years. Great game 10/10 on experience just sucks I paid 20 bucks for less an hour and 4 levels.
Hope have more like this. Great mind twisted game.

Playing in room scale the entire game feels completely seamless. The puzzles aren't too challenging, but utilize lots of clever devices that are a delight to interact with. There's nothing on the quest like this and you should experience it for yourself (provided you have the room)

reviews.review_title: This is my new VR showcase-game!
reviews.review_description:
And for good reason, this is stellar roomscale experience, that do not require more than 2x2m which is perfect. I always thought the walking in vr was fun in tea for god but here its more cleverly layd out because its not procedural randomness, but more cleverly designed areas that feel much bigger although you can't walk anywhere. It's a very trippy and atmospheric puzzle game combined with physical walking. It's all very very clever. I've played a couple of these impossible spaces games before, but this on is by far the smoothest and best I've tried. It's on the shorter side, and the puzzles are quite easy.. but i don't mind that at all. It's the traversal and mystery that's the driving factor in this game.
And I'm glad there's no zombie hunting for me in the hallway, that's a breath of fresh air to just walk, solve and explore. 

I would love a way to adjust the 2x2 squares rotation and position though, sometimes you  clean the floor for things but the square is not exactly where you  think it will be. 

Now I hope you make more! Longer levels, more backtracking, endless mode? I'm all for this, great job to all of you! 🙌

reviews.review_title: Excellent immersion in room scale VR!
reviews.review_description: My wife and I just finished playing through Hotel Infinity and we had a blast!  The way the rooms and hallways of the hotel are laid out keeps you twisting and turning, but since you are actually moving your body around it is a very comfortable experience.  There are definitely some moments in the game where you forget you are safe in your play space and not teetering on the brink of disaster.  Great work from the development team!

reviews.review_title: Enjoying it. *update stuck at end of ch4
reviews.review_description: Got this on release day since I'm a fan of the designer's other work. I don't have room scale space, the stationary mode worked well. Neat visuals, good execution, puzzles are pretty straightforward but to be honest I prefer that for this sort of mainly aesthetic experience. *Update / issue note: at the end of ch 4 there's a situation I assume is meant to be at the end of the level, it's visually climactic, but gameplay wise very anticlimactic because I've been stuck for several minutes unsure what to do. The mini building has handles which worked for the first few, but seem entirely unresponsive for the last two. I may just be so stuck I have to quit?

developer_response.body: Hi there, thank you for the feedback and sorry to hear you're stuck. If I'm understanding correctly, you're stuck at the part where you have to pull apart the miniature building. The pieces do go in a variety of different directions, and also have to be done in a certain order. The grab points also overlap a bit somewhat, so it's possible you were grabbing the second one when trying to grab the first... You can go to the Chapter Select menu and select Chapter 5 to try it again. Make sure you're in build 26161. If you're continuing to have issues, please feel free to send an email to will@studiochyr.com and I can help you resolve it.

reviews.review_description:
I don’t often play in VR. 

But a game by the people who made Manifold Garden?

Featuring a cool looking hotel inspired by Chicago hotels and a strange vibe. 

None of the puzzles are hard, they are fun moments to play and do things outside of looking at the cool views. 

This is essentially a great demo of what VR does with incredible vibes. 

It’s not that long.. but it’s the right length.

When I saw the trailer a long time ago, I was like, "Wow!" The way this game is designed with 2x2 real-world areas is genius! A completely immersive MR game! I only played for 15 minutes, and the experience was exactly what I expected! I'll definitely try it out more after get off work today! And my colleagues are all very interested too! We've decided to make a compilation of different colleagues' reactions! They'll be amazed!! Thank you to the development team! Love you all!

Expect an experience and not a game and you will be treated to a very rewarding wonder-filled time with some truly cool moments. It is short but great to share. If you are scared of heights beware that Chapter 4 will need you to get over it to complete the experience.

I love the game I've been looking forward to games like this do you games when you walk around physically is one of my favorite games that makes it feel more realistic as if you was actually there and the whole joystick thing you should not added that cuz when you use the joystick and then you decide to walk again it messes up your walking ability and it kind of takes a fun out of it when you use a joystick when it's meant to be walked please add more to the game if you can I can see this game potential going for a lot of them don't make games like this so I'm happy that you made a game like this you go the type of games I love to play when I can physically walk around in the environment and interact it makes it more realistic than using a joystick kind of kills it but overall the game is fun I haven't had no issues out of it I was excited when it released please add more if you can please make more games like this it makes it real fun when you physically walk keep up the good work love the game Make more like this please Y'all did it amazing job creedence game either talk to games I love playing makes me feel realistic like I'm not clean this hotel for real

Downloaded, planned to play roomscale (not expecting a controller mode so was very happy when this appeared)

Selected controller mode,

Eventually got through the spinning entrance door (not sure if thats how its intended to work but it was like the door weighed 100 tonne.

Followed the path around to where I opened 2 doors and could not get through.

It was as if there was an invisible wall unless I went through side ways which in turn didnt allow my left hand to come through with me for some odd reason.

I eventually manoeuvred my way to see the gold bell but I now was again facing an invisible wall where I could move next to the desk and both hands had vanished.

Amazing start... this will most likely be refunded very soon.

developer_response.body:
Hey Beatz, sorry to hear you had issues. 

Ultimately, the game was designed from the ground up to be played in roomscale. That's the way we intended the game to be experienced. We did add controller mode, but mostly as an accessibility feature. I will be upfront and say that controller mode is nowhere near as good as the roomscale, and perhaps we shouldn't have added that as option in the first place. 

Would you mind trying the game once more but in roomscale, as you had originally planned, and let me know how your experience is? I'd love to hear your thoughts in that mode. It really is completely different. 

We do plan on pushing a patch that will address some of the most critical issues with joystick movement, like loss of tracking. However, at the end of the day, the game really was not designed with that in mind.

reviews.review_description: Just put a good amount of time into the game and I’m thoroughly impressed. I feel like it hasn’t been very difficult, but the puzzles are still very fun and satisfying to solve. The environments look great as well. Some bugs here and there with my hands duplicating where they shouldn’t be, but overall it’s very fun so far.
Really enjoyed this. Makes great use of room scale VR. Really hoping for some DLC!

Just a delight. This is exactly the sort of experience that can only happen in VR. I moved some furniture in my living room to give myself a fully unobstructed 2x2m and it was absolutely worth it. After playing so much artificial locomotion in VR, it feels like a revelation to explore a space with just my body again. I hope more folks dive in and give this game a shot. This is the sort of thing one should use to convert folks over to being VR Gamers :-)

I saw some mediocre reviews and was very sceptical. But this really is a  masterpiece. I love the art style. The game is both funny and kind of creepy at the same time. You have to play it in roomscale to fully admire its greatness.

It's amazing to walk naturally in Vr, this is real exploration in an amazing Hotel. Very charming and a good show stopper for new players. But a little bit longer would be nice, an add on like the swimming pool or the laundry.

Really cool experience if you have the square footage to physically walk around your play   Space. Unfortunately the puzzles mostly involve pushing buttons and pulling levers to trigger admittedly very trippy and mind bending visuals.

reviews.review_title: Entire game feels like a cutscene
reviews.review_description: The visuals are interesting, kinda, but it never really felt like there was anything to figure out. Walk through the only available path, grab object, move it the only place it goes, repeat. I really liked manifold garden (creator's previous work) which had a large somewhat confusing world, puzzles with surprising mechanics that required taking some time to understand the world, really beautiful visuals.... Hotel Infinity has none of those. I suspect it just sucks because VR as a medium is so limiting, you can only do things that don't make the player barf, you have to fit everything in a 2m square, limited movement dexterity... maybe figuring out how VR works at all is so hard that it for many players it's enough of a puzzle leaving no room to add any actual puzzles without making the game too hard.

Updating my review to five stars :) they fixed the colliders. 

I like the game the art direction is beautiful and the interactions are quite fun and well made. Some imperfections:
- cannot save the game and have to restart a whole chapter on reload.
-fixed. whenever I recenter the view all hell break loose and I end up clipping everywhere and in everything, and the only way to fix it is to finish or reload, which as indicated above loses all progress, this is hugely annoying not to have proper recenter.
- there is no remote grab and items sometimes fall on the floor, since the real floor never properly aligns with the game floor it's almost always impossible to pick up items on the floor.

Beyond these useabiltiy issue the game is fun

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the review and the feedback!

We definitely want to take a look at the re-center view issue and the floor alignment problem. Were you playing in roomscale or stationary mode?

reviews.review_description: Wish there were more puzzles. Completed within an hour and puzzles were fun, but not very complex. Overall fun, but tough decision to pay for.
The concept is really great, but why do the visuals have to be C=64-like? Q3 can handle much much better visuals.  This game could be so much better with some eye candy…

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback and thank you for playing! Ultimately, we had to find the right balance between performance and visuals, while still allowing for fast iteration from a development side. I also wish we could have really pushed the graphics even further! We do plan on submitting a patch to address key issues like flickering and assets popping in and out to make the game even smoother visually.

reviews.review_description: Love the experience. I had a 2x2 space and the room scale was quite immersive.
Excellent movement mechanics, great puzzles, creepy vibes, an overall awesome game.
If you have enough playing space, and you like games with movement similar to Tea For God, The single hotel I recommend for the upcoming holiday is Hotel Infinity

reviews.review_title: I love impossible space apps like this!
reviews.review_description: I was so excited to find out that this is what it was. The best one so far is Tea for God, but this one does a good job of it, too. More of these please!
Need more games like this in VR

The game is good just Patch the issues 

Flickering 

The graphics need a bit of work
One of the best liminal space I experienced in VR!

reviews.review_title: A brief visit to a creepy hotel
reviews.review_description: Good game but way too short!
Hoping for Quest Achievements in a future update.

This game is a lot of fun. I just bought it and was testing it out. But does it not save your progress? Seriously?  I mean if someone needs to take a break or pause the game they have to start over?  That seems like a huge oversight. Or is that part of the challenge?

developer_response.body:
Hi Sally, the game saves progress between the chapters. The main reason we implemented this way is because for roomscale, the calibration sequence happens at the beginning of the chapter, and that's necessary in order to position the player in the correct physical location. 

After you've completed a chapter, if you quit, you should be able to access the next chapter in the "Chapter Select" screen in the menu. If you're still having issues, please send an email to me at will@studiochyr.com and I help you sort it out.
